title: Parking Lot Speed Limit — 5 MPH, Watch for Children
body: For the safety of all residents, please be advised that a strict speed limit of 5 miles per hour is in effect throughout the building's parking lots and driveways at all times. Children are frequently present in these areas, and drivers must remain alert, proceed slowly, and yield to pedestrians at all times. Operators of all vehicles are required to exercise the utmost caution when entering, exiting, and maneuvering within the lots. Failure to observe the posted speed limit endangers the community and may result in the issuance of a formal violation notice. The Board thanks all residents for their cooperation in keeping our community safe.

title: Pet Waste Policy — Please Clean Up After Your Pet
body: All residents and their guests are hereby reminded that pet owners are required to clean up after their pets at all times, whether indoors or outdoors. This obligation applies to all common areas, including but not limited to hallways, elevators, lobbies, stairwells, walkways, and the grounds of the property. Failure to promptly remove and properly dispose of pet waste constitutes a violation of the building's rules and regulations, and may result in the issuance of a formal violation notice and the imposition of applicable penalties. The Board thanks all residents for their cooperation in maintaining a clean, safe, and sanitary environment for the entire community.

title: Scheduled Hot Water Interruption — June 16 & 17
body: Please be advised that hot water service will be temporarily interrupted on Tuesday, June 16 and Wednesday, June 17, 2026, between approximately 9:00 AM and 3:00 PM each day. This work is necessary to complete post-winter maintenance on the building's boiler system. Hot water service will be restored as promptly as possible once each day's work is complete. We thank residents for their patience and apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

title: Damaged Tree Near Garage Entrance — Safety Alert
body: Residents, please be aware that a tree section near the dumpster area at the end of the driveway has sustained damage and is currently resting against neighboring branches and overhead Verizon lines. The Fire Department visited the site this evening and assessed the situation. Because the tree sits beyond our fence line as part of the aqueduct property, responsibility for removal falls outside our jurisdiction. We have already reached out to the county Department of Public Works and are awaiting their direction on next steps. In the meantime, we ask everyone to take extra care when pulling in or out of the garage and when passing through that area on foot. Your safety is our priority. We will keep you updated as the situation develops.

title: Welcome to the 555 McLean Ave Resident Portal
body: This website is your central resource for building news, meeting schedules, and community announcements. Bookmark this page and check back regularly for updates.

title: Spring Building Inspection
body: Our annual spring inspection of common areas, roof, and mechanical systems is underway. Access to certain areas may be temporarily restricted. Thank you for your patience.

title: Maintenance Fee Payment Reminder
body: Monthly maintenance fees are due on the 1st of each month. Please ensure payments are submitted on time to avoid late charges. Contact the managing agent with any questions.
